etymology.description: 好(好) (hǎo, "good") and 的(的) (de, "a structural particle").
etymology.interpretation: 好(好) (hǎo, "good") conveys approval or affirmation, indicating something is satisfactory. 的(的) (de, "a structural particle") often modifies a noun or adjective, here creating a phrase that affirms or agrees with what has been said, similar to saying "it's good" or "it's okay" in English.

parts.english: a structural particle; possessive marker; used to form adjectives

examples.chinese: 你明天能来吗(你明天能來嗎)？
好的，我会准时到(好的，我會準時到)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎo de, wǒ huì zhǔnshí dào.
examples.english: Ok, I will arrive on time.

examples.chinese: 我们去吃饭吧(我們去吃飯吧)，好的(好的)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qù chīfàn ba, hǎo de.
